description: Cautilus claim-fidelity specs: does a real /charness:<skill> run honor its own reference-routing claims. A skill may register multiple scenario fixtures (default scenario at spec.json; named branches like setup greenfield/normalization at <scenario>.spec.json with a scenario_id). See charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md.

specs.fan_out_fit: yes — critique has two representative runs: the bare/autonomous self-activation path (this default fixture; deterministic RCF proves the path was followed, inference quality is the Cautilus-recommendation signal) and a pinned-target path (decision.spec.json).

yes — pinned decision premortem makes premortem-decision.md unconditional, so the deterministic tooth (premortem-decision) carries the weight here, complementing the autonomous fixture. (counterweight-triage.md retired from RCF in the Slice-7 reconciliation: census INLINE, and here it passed only via a subagent-prompt name-mention, #415.)

yes — handoff branches by intent (plan_handoff_run.py _required_reads are intent-keyed and mutually exclusive); this default scenario is the chunked_routing (bare) intent whose single required read is chunked-routing.md.
yes — the pickup intent reads workflow-trigger.md + continuation-sequence.md; a chunked_routing or refresh run opens neither, so requiring them needs its own scenario.
yes — the refresh intent reads state-selection.md + spill-targets.md, mutually exclusive with the pickup and chunked_routing reads, so its own scenario.

yes — issue branches by intent (issue_tool.py plan emits intent-keyed required_reads); the new intent's unconditional reads are [issue-shaping, issue-backend, closeout-discipline], mutually exclusive with the resolve intent's resolve-flow read.
yes — the resolve intent's unconditional reads are [resolve-flow, issue-backend, closeout-discipline]; a new-intent run opens none of resolve-flow, so requiring it needs its own scenario.
yes — setup branches greenfield vs normalization on detected repo mode (mutually exclusive flows), so each branch is its own claim-fidelity scenario rather than one over-broad fixture.
yes — normalization is the in-repo-capturable branch (a mature repo like charness detects PARTIAL/NORMALIZE, never greenfield); the greenfield branch needs a fresh-repo sandbox to capture.
